latex: When Cedric walked into a party, two-thirds of those invited had already arrived. Six more people arrived just after Cedric, bringing the number at the party to $\frac{5}{6}$ of those invited. What was the total number of invited guests?
notes: }  \textbf{Answer:} Let $P$ be the number of people invited to the party. Before Cedric arrived, there were $\frac{2}{3}P$ people at the party. After Cedric and six others arrived, there are $\frac{2}{3}P+7$ people at the party. Since this is the same as $\frac{5}{6}P$, we solve $\frac{2}{3}P+7=\frac{5}{6}P$ to find that $P=\boxed{42}$. }
